creator: peter@spinner.DIALix.COM
text:
root@aldan.ziplink.net wrote:
> >Description:
> 
> 	1. `make patch' fails -- most of the correction are already made
> 	in the source, but the version number remains same.

"It works for me!" :-)

> 	2. If the name of the client and/or server changed from default
> 	in mf/Makefile.var -- post-install fails, because names
> 	`rdist6' and `rdistd' are hardcoded in the port's Makefile.

"Doctor! It hurts when I do this!" - "Well, don't do it then!" :-)

It's the same for the man pages and packages, the package plist assumes a 
standard system without NOMANCOMPRESS active.  if you change 
mf/Makefile.var by hand, then you should (obviously :-) change the wrapper 
Makefile and package list since it won't uninstall properly otherwise.

> 	3. Install-directory specified in mf/Makefile.var (/usr/ucb):
> 		a) does not exist;
> 		b) shoud be /usr/local/bin and or /usr/local/libexec
> 	4. `make install' does not install the man-pages (seems like
> 	a bug in rdist6's Makefiles), but attempts to compress them.

Huh?  I do not see any of this... See attatched script..

> 	5. Compiler builds with `-g' option by default, instead of
> 	whatever is specified in /usr/share/mk/sys.mk (Common problem
> 	for other ports as well).

It doesn't for me.  Have you set "-g" in "CFLAGS" in /etc/make.conf?

> >How-To-Repeat:
> 
> 	cd /usr/ports/net/rdist6
> 	make patch
> 	make install

'make patch' is redundant, since the .<stage>_done files make sure that 
the previous stage is complete before proceding. ie: 'make install' will 
fetch, extract, patch, build, and install.

> >Fix:
> 	
> 	make patch
> 	touch work/.patch_done
> 
> 	Then deal with other problems manually.

I think this is the root cause of the problems. If the patches are 
failing, you need to find out why, rather than hotwiring the patch stage.

peter@spinner[12:25pm]~ports/net/rdist6-126> cvs -q update
? work
